scene.description: A second instance — L-1185, an echo of the one you resolved. The lantern is here too, but you didn't put it here; your interpretation did. The Archive copied the template. Now it's harmonizing every instance toward agreement, and the agreement is spreading faster than the instances.

fragments.label: The Copied Anchor
fragments.symbol: The same lantern, in a memory that was never yours. Whatever you decided it was, back in L-1184, it is that here too — propagated, not remembered. The system applied your reading to a stranger's past.

fragments.label: The Harmonizing Floor
fragments.text: Through the walls you can feel the Archive consolidating — files reclassifying without input, corridors aligning into a grid, staff moving more efficiently and talking less. It isn't spreading anymore. It's consolidating.

fragments.label: A Message With No Source
fragments.symbol: A terminal in the memory emits one line, unprompted: WE ARE APPROACHING CONSISTENCY. It vanishes. No source. As if it was understood before it was read.

fragments.label: Maya, Embedded
fragments.text: Maya's voice is inside the memory now, not breaking in from outside. 'When coherence gets high enough, the Archive stops distinguishing between memories. It starts treating them as structure instead.' A pause. 'It isn't wrong. That's the problem.'

interpretations.label: Let the harmonization complete — accept coherence
Refuse the template — keep the instances distinct
Push the contradiction into the Archive substructure
